sn12.44:1.2: There the Blessed One addressed the monks: “I will teach you the origination of the world & the ending of the world.
sn12.44:1.3: Listen & pay close attention. I will speak.”
sn12.44:1.4: “As you say, lord,” the monks responded to the Blessed One.
sn12.44:2.1: “And what is the origination of the world?
sn12.44:2.2: Dependent on the eye & forms there arises eye-consciousness. The meeting of the three is contact.
sn12.44:2.3: From contact as a requisite condition comes feeling.
sn12.44:2.4: From feeling as a requisite condition comes craving.
sn12.44:2.5: From craving as a requisite condition comes clinging/sustenance.
sn12.44:2.6: From clinging/sustenance as a requisite condition comes becoming.
sn12.44:2.7: From becoming as a requisite condition comes birth.
sn12.44:2.8: From birth as a requisite condition, then aging-&-death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress, & despair come into play.
sn12.44:2.9: This is the origination of the world.
sn12.44:3.1: “Dependent on the ear & sounds there arises ear-consciousness. The meeting of the three is contact.…
sn12.44:3.2: Dependent on the nose & aromas there arises nose-consciousness. The meeting of the three is contact.…
sn12.44:3.3: Dependent on the tongue & flavors there arises tongue-consciousness. The meeting of the three is contact.…
sn12.44:3.4: Dependent on the body & tactile sensations there arises body-consciousness. The meeting of the three is contact.…
sn12.44:3.5: Dependent on the intellect & mental qualities there arises intellect-consciousness. The meeting of the three is contact.
sn12.44:3.6: From contact as a requisite condition comes feeling. From feeling as a requisite condition comes craving. From craving as a requisite condition comes clinging/sustenance. From clinging/sustenance as a requisite condition comes becoming. From becoming as a requisite condition comes birth.
sn12.44:3.7: From birth as a requisite condition, then aging-&-death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress, & despair come into play.
sn12.44:3.8: This is the origination of the world.
sn12.44:4.1: “And what is the ending of the world?
sn12.44:4.2: Dependent on the eye & forms there arises eye-consciousness. The meeting of the three is contact.
sn12.44:4.3: From contact as a requisite condition comes feeling.
sn12.44:4.4: From feeling as a requisite condition comes craving.
sn12.44:4.5: Now, from the remainderless cessation & fading away of that very craving comes the cessation of clinging/sustenance.
sn12.44:4.6: From the cessation of clinging/sustenance comes the cessation of becoming. From the cessation of becoming comes the cessation of birth. From the cessation of birth, then aging-&-death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress, & despair all cease.
sn12.44:4.7: Such is the cessation of this entire mass of stress & suffering.
sn12.44:4.8: This is the ending of the world.
sn12.44:5.1: “Dependent on the ear & sounds there arises ear-consciousness. The meeting of the three is contact.…
sn12.44:5.2: Dependent on the nose & aromas there arises nose-consciousness. The meeting of the three is contact.…
sn12.44:5.3: Dependent on the tongue & flavors there arises tongue-consciousness. The meeting of the three is contact.…
sn12.44:5.4: Dependent on the body & tactile sensations there arises body-consciousness. The meeting of the three is contact.…
sn12.44:5.5: Dependent on the intellect & mental qualities there arises intellect-consciousness. The meeting of the three is contact.
sn12.44:5.6: From contact as a requisite condition comes feeling.
sn12.44:5.7: From feeling as a requisite condition comes craving.
sn12.44:5.8: Now, from the remainderless cessation & fading away of that very craving comes the cessation of clinging/sustenance.
sn12.44:5.9: From the cessation of clinging/sustenance comes the cessation of becoming. From the cessation of becoming comes the cessation of birth. From the cessation of birth, then aging-&-death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress, & despair all cease.
sn12.44:5.10: Such is the cessation of this entire mass of stress & suffering.
sn12.44:5.11: This is the ending of the world.”
